record.field: Blinko is an open-source, self-hosted AI note-taking tool that pairs frictionless quick capture with AI-powered semantic search.

Blinko is an open-source, self-hosted AI note-taking tool that pairs frictionless quick capture with AI-powered semantic search.

Blinko separates fleeting thoughts ("Blinkos") from polished, permanent "Notes", so a personal knowledge base stays organized instead of cluttered. Built with TypeScript, Next.js and PostgreSQL, it offers optional Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) backed by providers like OpenAI or Ollama, letting users find notes by meaning rather than keywords while keeping all data on their own infrastructure. It is aimed at privacy-conscious individuals who want a fast, modern personal knowledge manager they fully control.

Key features
  • Dual capture model: quick "Blinkos" promoted to structured "Notes" with tags
  • Optional AI semantic search (RAG) via OpenAI or Ollama, off by default
  • Full Markdown editing with public sharing of selected notes
  • Self-hosted with PostgreSQL and scheduled database backups
  • Cross-platform clients (macOS, Windows, Linux, Android) plus a quick-capture desktop snap shortcut
  • RSS reader and note import/export for a portable knowledge base
Best for

Individuals and privacy-focused teams who want a self-hosted personal knowledge manager for idea capture, daily notes and AI-assisted retrieval without surrendering data to a cloud service.

field.name: tag_ids
field.eval: [Command.set([ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_self_hosted'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_notes'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_ai'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_productivity'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_documentation'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_search'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_privacy')])]

record.field: services: blinko-website: image: blinkospace/blinko:1.6.3 environment: NODE_ENV: production NEXTAUTH_SECRET: {{ nextauth_secret }} DATABASE_URL: {{ database_url }} NEXTAUTH_URL: {{ nextauth_url }} NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_URL: {{ next_public_base_url }} depends_on: blinko-postgres: condition: service_healthy restart: always logging: options: max-size: 10m max-file: '3' ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:1111' container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network blinko-postgres: image: postgres:14 restart: always environment: POSTGRES_DB: postgres POSTGRES_USER: postgres POSTGRES_PASSWORD: {{ postgres_password }} TZ: Asia/Shanghai volumes: - ./blinko_db:/var/lib/postgresql/data healthcheck: test: - CMD - pg_isready - -U - postgres - -d - postgres interval: 5s timeout: 10s retries: 5 container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_blinko_postgres' networks: - internal networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Dump consistente de PostgreSQL (servicio en marcha) → $BACKUP_DATA_DIR docker compose exec -T blinko-postgres sh -c 'pg_dump -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --no-owner -Fc' \ > "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump" echo "pg_dump OK ($(wc -c < "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump") bytes)"

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Levanta solo la DB, espera readiness y restaura docker compose up -d blinko-postgres for i in $(seq 1 30); do docker compose exec -T blinko-postgres sh -c 'pg_isready -U "$POSTGRES_USER"' && break sleep 2 done docker compose exec -T blinko-postgres sh -c 'pg_restore -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --clean --if-exists --no-owner' \ < "$RESTORE_DATA_DIR/db.dump" # Verificación: la DB no quedó vacía (sh -c con dobles comillas; $VAR del contenedor con \$) N=$(docker compose exec -T blinko-postgres sh -c "psql -U \$POSTGRES_USER -d \$POSTGRES_DB -tAc \"SELECT count(*) FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema='public'\"") echo "tablas restauradas: $N" test "$N" -gt 0
